title: Broken Access Control

explain.what: A user can access data or actions that belong to another user or require higher privileges. The server checks who is logged in, but not what they are allowed to touch.
explain.example: You log in as Alice. Your invoice is at /invoice/1. You change it to /invoice/2 and read Bob's private $2,500 invoice.
explain.impact: Data theft · privilege escalation · account takeover · #1 most common vulnerability in OWASP 2026

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ The server confirms you are logged in (x-user-id header) but never checks whether the invoice belongs to you.

fixed.annotation: ✅ After fetching the invoice, we verify the owner matches the logged-in user. Mismatches return 403 Forbidden.

demoHint: Log in as Alice (user 1), then change the invoice ID in the URL to 2 or 3 to read Bob's and Admin's private invoices.
